cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out this problem step by step. So, there are five golfers in a tournament: Amy, Mel, Rob, Joe, and Ada. We need to determine their finishing positions based on the given clues and then figure out which of the options is correct. First, let me list out the clues provided in the paragraph: 1. Joe finished second. 2. Joe finished below Amy. 3. Mel finished second-to-last. 4. Ada finished last. Wait, the first clue says Joe finished second. But then there's another clue that says Joe finished below Amy. If Joe is second, then Amy must be above him. So Amy must be first, right? Because if Joe is second, then Amy has to be first to be above him. That makes sense so far. Next, Mel finished second-to-last. Second-to-last in a five-person tournament would be the fourth position. Because last is fifth, so second-to-last is fourth. So Mel is in position 4. Ada finished last, which is fifth position.
